reason: The item cleanly tests boundary-aware, multi-metric hazard reasoning (ignition delay regime vs. overpressure sensitivity vs. LBV non-monotonicity) and rewards resisting an unqualified “equal hazard reduction” claim. Decoys are largely plausible and map to common reviewer errors (regime misclassification, rank-based dismissal, proxy conflation), though two incorrect options lean on similar mechanism-confusion patterns, slightly reducing discriminative sharpness.

The item cleanly targets boundary-aware engineering judgment about why an LFP-calibrated LBV trigger is not automatically conservative for NMC and why lean-regime behavior can dominate overpressure risk before a lean-to-stoichiometric threshold is crossed. It offers strong diagnostic value by requiring two distinct flaw-mechanism links (chemistry-dependent flame speed ranking and equivalence-ratio-dependent overpressure sensitivity), though neutrality could be improved because the prompt telegraphs the expected critique and the short-answer format may admit partially correct responses with fuzzy boundary handling.
